pattern_recognition: Linked List Manipulation, as this problem requires understanding of pointers and node relationships. Signals include reversing the order of nodes.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Create a new linked list and insert nodes in reverse order.
approaches.algorithm: 1. Initialize a new linked list. 2. Traverse the original list and insert each node at the head of the new list.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Reverse the pointers in the existing linked list without using extra space.
approaches.algorithm: 1. Initialize three pointers: previous, current, and next. 2. Iterate through the list, adjusting pointers to reverse the links.

dry_run: Input: 1 2 3 4 5. Start with head pointing to 1. Initialize previous as null. Current points to head. While current is not null, set next to current.next, current.next to previous, then move previous to current and current to next. Repeat until current is null. The new head will be previous, which points to 5.
edge_cases: Empty input results in null. A single element list remains unchanged. Duplicates are handled as normal nodes. Large inputs should be tested for performance. Overflow is not a concern since we are only reversing pointers.
common_pitfalls: 1. Forgetting to update the next pointer can lead to losing access to the rest of the list. 2. Not handling the case of an empty list can cause null pointer exceptions. 3. Incorrectly setting the new head can result in returning the wrong list.
